recommended_paths: Fix the cooldown semantics and user-scope alignment before another audit round, then decide whether the recovery-contract documentation gap should remain as non-blocking debt.
exact_next_step: Record reviewer resolutions in the governing TODO using the machine-checkable resolution table or equivalent gate ledger, then extract the derived resolution packet and decide whether another bounded review pass is still required.

findings.title: Reset-issue cooldown is consumed before issuance success is known
findings.rationale: At review time the reset issue path acquired the cooldown before token issuance had definitively succeeded and did not reliably roll it back if issuance failed. That can create a false suppression window and force unnecessary retries under error conditions.
findings.suggested_action: Ensure cooldown acquisition is scoped to successful issuance or explicitly released on issuance failure.

findings.title: Cooldown suppression is email-scoped while token invalidation is user-scoped
findings.rationale: The reset lifecycle invalidates older tokens per user, but the cooldown at review time followed the email subject only. That leaves alias-rotation space where one user with multiple email subjects can bypass the intended reset issuance ceiling.
findings.suggested_action: Align the reset issuance cooldown with the same user-scoped identity used for token invalidation.

findings.title: Reissue-required reset recovery contract is not promoted into canonical docs
findings.rationale: The slice already behaves as burn-before-mutate with fresh reissue required after post-consume mutation failure, but that recovery contract is not yet promoted clearly enough into the canonical authority surfaces.
findings.suggested_action: Promote the reissue-required recovery contract into the TODO/package/module authority surfaces or accept the documentation gap as low debt.
